Title: SiMYB19 from Foxtail Millet (Setaria italica) Confers Transgenic Rice Tolerance to High Salt Stress in the Field

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Functional studies were performed via heterologous expression of foxtail millet SiMYB19 in rice; no SiMYB19 loss-of-function mutants in foxtail millet are reported. The salt tolerance index formula and classification are explicitly defined and used to compare WT and OE-6 performance under field salinity.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. SiMYB19 (Seita.1G250600.1) is an R2R3-MYB transcription factor from foxtail millet, located on chromosome 1, with three exons and two introns and two SANT conserved domains.
2. SiMYB19 is predominantly expressed in roots and is strongly induced by salt (NaCl), osmotic stress (PEG), low nitrogen, and ABA, and weakly by IAA.
3. SiMYB19 protein localizes to the nucleus and functions as a transcriptional activator in yeast, consistent with a role as a transcription factor.
4. Heterologous overexpression of SiMYB19 in rice (OE-6, OE-8, OE-9) significantly enhances seedling salt tolerance in the greenhouse, with higher survival, growth, and lower MDA under 100 mM NaCl.
5. In field and salt-pond trials, SiMYB19-overexpressing line OE-6 shows greater plant height, grain yield per plant, and overall salt tolerance under 0.3% and 0.5% NaCl than WT.
6. SiMYB19 overexpression increases ABA accumulation and ABA sensitivity in rice; ABA-related phenotypes are abolished by tungstate, indicating involvement of ABA biosynthesis.
7. Expression of ABA biosynthesis gene OsNCED3, ABA signaling genes OsPK1 and OsABF2, and stress-related gene OsLEA7 is upregulated in SiMYB19 transgenic rice under control and salt conditions.
8. OE-6 exhibits improved drought tolerance in soil-drought experiments, suggesting that SiMYB19 mediates broader abiotic stress tolerance through an ABA-dependent pathway.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of foxtail millet subjected to drought and salt stress was used previously to identify SiMYB19 as a stress-responsive MYB transcription factor; no accession numbers are provided in this article.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SiMYB19 overexpression in rice enhances tolerance to high salinity at germination and seedling stages, reduces oxidative damage, and increases plant height and grain yield of field-grown rice under saline conditions.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Nuclear R2R3-MYB transcriptional activator that promotes ABA accumulation and ABA-dependent stress responses by increasing expression of ABA biosynthesis gene OsNCED3 and ABA signaling components OsPK1 and OsABF2, leading to upregulation of downstream stress-protective gene OsLEA7.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: ABA-dependent stress signaling pathway regulating salt and drought tolerance via increased ABA biosynthesis (NCED3) and ABA signal transduction (PK1, ABF2), leading to induction of stress-protective genes (e.g., LEA).
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Regulation of ABA biosynthesis (via upregulation of OsNCED3) under salt stress
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB transcription factor containing two SANT conserved domains
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus (tentative); 35S:GFP-SiMYB19 fusion protein localized to the nucleus in foxtail millet mesophyll protoplasts.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In foxtail millet, SiMYB19 is mainly expressed in roots and is strongly induced by NaCl, osmotic stress (10% PEG6000), low nitrogen (0.2 mM NO3-), and ABA, and weakly induced by IAA; in transgenic rice, SiMYB19 is highly expressed in OE lines (OE-6, OE-8, OE-9) and remains inducible by salt.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salt stress (100 mM NaCl in hydroponics; 0.3% and 0.5% NaCl in field and 0.3% NaCl in salt ponds; 100–250 mM NaCl during seed germination), osmotic stress (10% PEG6000), low nitrogen (0.2 mM NO3-), exogenous ABA (100 μM), auxin (100 μM IAA), ABA synthesis inhibitor tungstate (5 mM), and soil drought (15 d water withholding followed by rewatering).
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Crop salt and drought tolerance improvement through transcription factor-mediated ABA pathway regulation.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (salt stress tolerance, drought tolerance, low-nitrogen response, ABA sensitivity).
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: In greenhouse 100 mM NaCl treatment, transgenic rice lines OE-6, OE-8, and OE-9 show seedling survival rates of 20–40% after recovery versus 5% in WT; plant height, root length, aboveground and underground fresh weight, and dry weight are all significantly higher in transgenic lines (p < 0.05 or p < 0.01), while MDA content is significantly lower (p < 0.05). In field trials under 0.3% NaCl, OE-6 exhibits significantly greater plant height and grain weight per plant than WT (p < 0.01). Salt tolerance indices at 0.3% NaCl are ~98% for WT and ~60% for OE-6, with the lower index corresponding to higher tolerance; the relative salt tolerance index of OE-6 is significantly lower than that of WT (p < 0.01).
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Overexpression in rice line OE-6 confers improved drought tolerance in soil (higher survival after 15 d water withholding and 5–7 d rewatering) and increases ABA sensitivity, as indicated by lower germination rates than WT under normal conditions and higher endogenous ABA content, effects abolished by the ABA synthesis inhibitor tungstate.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Foxtail millet (Setaria italica) cultivar Yugu1; Oryza sativa cv. Kitaake (WT) and SiMYB19-overexpressing transgenic lines OE-6, OE-8, OE-9; foxtail millet mesophyll protoplasts; yeast strain AH109.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: RNA-seq-based candidate identification; qRT-PCR expression profiling under NaCl, PEG, low nitrogen, ABA, and IAA; subcellular localization using 35S:GFP-SiMYB19 transient expression in foxtail millet mesophyll protoplasts; yeast one-hybrid transcriptional activation assay in AH109; Agrobacterium-mediated transformation of Oryza sativa cv. Kitaake with pCAMBIA1390-SiMYB19; greenhouse salt-stress assays (100 mM NaCl) at seedling stage; field and salt-pond salt-tolerance assays under 0.3% and 0.5% NaCl; seed germination assays under 100–250 mM NaCl and 5 mM tungstate; ABA quantification by ELISA; soil drought-stress assay; qRT-PCR of ABA-pathway and stress-related genes OsNCED3, OsPK1, OsABF2, and OsLEA7.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional validation by Agrobacterium-mediated overexpression in rice (Kitaake) and assessment of salt tolerance in greenhouse, field, and salt ponds; germination assays under graded NaCl concentrations; ABA sensitivity and ABA content measurements with and without tungstate; soil drought-tolerance assay; subcellular localization via GFP fusion in foxtail millet protoplasts; transcription activation assay in yeast; qRT-PCR analysis of ABA-pathway and stress-related genes.
Plant_Genes.Field_Trials: Field experiments with SiMYB19-overexpressing rice line OE-6 and WT (Kitaake) under control (water), 0.3% (w/v) NaCl, and 0.5% (w/v) NaCl irrigation; salt concentrations maintained by monitoring electrical conductivity (~6 mS/cm for 0.3% and ~10 mS/cm for 0.5% NaCl). Plant height, yield per plant, salt tolerance index, and relative salt tolerance index were measured at maturity. Complementary salt-pond trials were conducted under control and 0.3% (w/v) NaCl to compare growth and salt tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: SiMYB19 provides a practical candidate gene for improving salt tolerance and yield in cereal crops cultivated on saline-alkali soils. Its overexpression in rice confers robust salt tolerance from germination through field growth and increases grain yield under 0.3% NaCl, demonstrating stable performance outside controlled environments. Integration of SiMYB19 into breeding programs could generate salt-tolerant, high-yielding germplasm suitable for saline-alkali land utilization, thereby helping to expand arable land and support food security; multi-year field trials are suggested to validate long-term agronomic stability.
